rulings.Case Title: ANDREW D FILIPEK VS. BRIAN DILL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice For Order To File Application For And Renewal Of Judgment Nunc Pro Tunc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on CALENDAR for FEB-07-2025, DEPT. 302, Line 11, PLAINTIFF ANDREW FILIPEK'S Notice For Order To File Application For And Renewal Of Judgment Nunc Pro Tunc. Off calendar. Moving party failed to lodge courtesy copies in compliance with SF Local Rule 2.7B. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: SAMEER BHADOURIA ET AL VS. MSASA PROPERITES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on CALENDAR for FEB-07-2025, DEPT. 302, Line 6, DEFENDANT MSASA PROPERTIES, LLC MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Defendant/Cross-complainant MSASA Properties, LLC's counsel Ashley E. Klein and Nixon Peabody LLC's motion to be relieved as counsel for Defendant/Cross-Complainant MSASA Properties, LLC. Defendant/Cross-Complainant MSASA Properties, LLC, through specially appearing counsel, filed an opposition. Plaintiff filed an opposition. The court will hold an in camera hearing at the time set for hearing this motion. Individuals seeking to participate must appear in person in Department 302 at the time of the hearing. A failure to appear at this time will be deemed a waiver or forfeiture of participation in the in camera proceeding. The court finds counsel Klein is not available for trial in April given her unanticipated, emergent, urgent and significant family health matter. Counsel of record, however, is Nixon Peabody, a large international, and other attorneys have appeared in this case . The court will hear about the alleged breakdown in the attorney-client relationship between counsel and Defendant/Cross-Complainant (specific occurrences and dates). The court will hear about the alleged breach of the engagement agreement by Defendant/Cross-Complainant (specific occurrences and dates). The court will hear about the time period between November 27, 2024, and January 15, 2025, and why Counsel Klein and Nixon Peabody did not immediately file their motion to withdraw following the November 27 hearing, as directed by the court, especially in light of the firm April 21, 2025, trial daten(specific occurrences and dates). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT MASJEDIAN VS. PATRICIA GUERRERO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Plaintiff To Vacate The December 20, 2024 Order Which Granted The Special Motion To Strike Of The Defendant Patricia Guerrero, Which Plaintiff Makes Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 663, As Well As 473(D); Notice Of Intention And Opening Brief; Request For Judicial Notice; Proposed Order;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on CALENDAR for FEB-07-2025, DEPT. 302, Line 10, PLAINTIFF ALBERT MASJEDIAN'S Motion To Vacate The December 20, 2024 Order Which Granted The Special Motion To Strike Of The Defendant Patricia Guerrero, Which Plaintiff Makes Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 663, As Well As 473(D); Notice Of Intention And Opening Brief; Request For Judicial Notice. Continued to be heard by Judge Hwang on Thursday, February 13, 2025, at 1:30 p.m. in department 502. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
